sales.title:
1787 Massachusetts Half Cent. Ryder 6-A, W-5970. Rarity-5+. EF Details--Scratch (PCGS).
79.3 grains. Bold antique copper-brown patina with a hint of olive. Both sides are well defined despite the presence of high point wear, the surfaces with some ancient build up nestled within the devices, although the only significant blemishes are a few old pin scratches at right obverse. This scarce variety was missing from the Taylor Collection. It is rare above the VF level, and only a single Mint State example is known, the fabulous Kendall-Martin specimen in PCGS MS-64 BN.
From the Richard August Collection.
PCGS# 688345. BASE PCGS# 296. NGC ID: 2B22.
